ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR RESPONDENT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO DEMURRER TO AMENDED PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION (FOR TENTATIVE RULING ONLY PART 2 OF 2) HEARING SET FOR MAY-04-2022 AT 09:30 AM IN DEPT 501

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, RESPONDENT'S DEMURRER TO AMENDED PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE IS OVERRULED, 30 DAYS TO ANSWER. BASED ON THE ALLEGATIONS IN THE OPERATIVE PETITION AND JUDICIALLY NOTICEABLE DOCUMENTS: (1) NO DECISION WITHIN THE MEANING OF GOVT CODE 66499.37 WAS MADE ON MARCH 2, 2021 ("CONDITIONALLY APPROVING THE DECISION, SUBJECT TO THE BOARD OF SUPERVISOR'S ADOPTION OF THE WRITTEN FINDINGS") AND NOTHING ON THE RECORD BEFORE THE COURT OR IN THE AUTHORITIES CITED BY THE MOVING PARTY SHOWS THAT THE UNCONDITIONAL/FINAL DECISION SUBJECT TO GOVT CODE PROVISIONS WAS COMMUNICATED IN ANY WAY TO THE PETITIONER 90+ DAYS BEFORE FILING AND SERVICE OF THE SUMMONS AND PETITION ON OCTOBER 6/7, 2021. ASSUMING THAT JUNE 29, 2021 WAS THE DATE OF THE DECISION CONTEMPLATED BY GOVT CODE 66499.37 (WITHOUT MAKE SUCH A DETERMINATION AT THIS TIME AS THERE ARE NO ALLEGATIONS OR JUDICIALLY NOTICEABLE DOCUMENTS INDICATING THAT PETITIONER WAS INFORMED OF THIS DECISION AND WHEN), THE COURT FINDS THAT PETITIONER IS ENTITLED TO EQUITABLE RELIEF FOR FAILURE TO TIMELY FILE AND SERVE THE PETITION; IT IS APPARENT FROM THE RECORD BEFORE THE COURT THAT THE DELAY IN FILING, ISSUANCE AND SERVICE OF SUMMONS WAS AT LEAST PARTIALLY CAUSED BY THE E-FILING REQUIREMENTS AND BACKLOG IN PROCESSING SUCH E-FILINGS. IN OTHER WORDS, HAD THE PETITIONER BEEN ALLOWED TO FILE THE PETITION AT THE WINDOW ON SEPTEMBER 23, 2021, THE PETITION WOULD HAVE BEEN FILED AND SUMMONS ISSUED ON SEPTEMBER 23, 2021 WITHIN 90 DAYS OF JUNE 29, 2021. SINCE SUMMONS WERE NOT ISSUED UNTIL OCTOBER 6, 2021, IT WAS NOT POSSIBLE FOR PETITIONER TO SERVE THE SUMMONS. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES, CLERK: N. WONG, REPORTED. (D501)

calendar.matters:
HEARING REGARDING PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE
Off Calendar Jun-16-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:

G. Scott Emblidge; Moscone Emblidge & Rubens LLP; 415-362-3599; appearing as counsel for Petitioners Adam Smith, et al.
Brian F. Crossman; Deputy City Attorney; 415-554-4690; appearing as counsel for Respondent City and County of San Francisco.

Counsel for Petitioners contests the court's tentative ruling. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

HEARING REGARDING PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE is DENIED. The Board's findings are supported by the record: that (1) conversion is accompanied by a 10-20% rent increase which provides somewhat increased housing accessibility to middle income residents.
(2) removal of these TIC units reduces the diversity of unit types and is inconsistent with Policy 3.3;
(3) removal of these TIC units reduces the diversity of unit types, and is inconsistent with Policy 5.4;
(4) conversion exacerbates the inaccessibility of homeownership in the North Beach neighborhood;
(5) the Tentative Map is not consistent with the General Plan;
(6) conversion does not promote the health, safety, and welfare of the City; and
(7) the Tentative Map application contained factual discrepancies, inconsistencies, and/or incorrect information regarding the building's short-term rental and eviction history.
Petitioners' equal protection argument fails. Petitioners did not establish that the existence of similarly situated persons, that the difference in treatment was intentional, or that there was no rational basis for the difference in treatment.
Respondent's request for judicial notice of Supervisors Ordinance No. 117-13, the Condominium Conversion Fee: Economic Impact Report, and San Francisco's Condominium Conversion Nexus Analysis is granted pursuant to Evidence Code sections 452(b), (h).
Petitioners' request for judicial notice of the General Plan, Housing Element, A copy of Owens v. City and County of San Francisco, Minutes of Planning Commission Hearing of September 24, 2020 and January 23, 2020, and the Planning Commissions Motions Nos. 20785 and 20634 are granted pursuant to Evidence Code sections 452(d), (h).

Order signed. Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Court reporter: Raquel Sharp, CSR #10619, raquel@transcriptlegal.com, 925-384-0003 (D501)

Respondent City And County Of San Francisco Demurrer To Amended Petition For Writ Of Mandate/ Prohibition/ Certification (For Tentativ Ruling Only Part 2 Of 2)
Off Calendar May-04-2022 Text Ruling

DEMURRER to Amended PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION
Off Calendar May-04-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
George Scott Emblidge, Esq., 415-362-3599, appearing for Adam Smith, Plaintiff
Brian Crossman, Esq., 415-584-4690, appearing for City and County of San Francisco, for Defendant.
Court reporter: Renee Sera. CSR #7435 T: (415)337-2077 E: reneesera@gmail.com.

ARGUED (as fully reported in the reporter's notes). Mr. Crossman presented his oral argument in support of his position. Mr. Emblidge responded to each issue presented in order.

The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the modified tentative ruling as follows:

Respondent's Demurrer to Amended Petition for Writ of Mandate is OVERRULED, 30 days to answer. Based on the allegations in the operative petition and judicially noticeable documents: (1) no decision within the meaning of Govt Code 66499.37 was made on March 2, 2021 ("conditionally approving the decision, subject to the Board of Supervisor's adoption of the written findings") and nothing on the record before the Court or in the authorities cited by the moving party shows that the unconditional/final decision subject to Govt Code provisions was communicated in any way to the Petitioner 90+ days before filing and service of the Summons and Petition on October 6/7, 2021. Assuming that June 29, 2021 was the date of the decision contemplated by Govt Code 66499.37 (without make such a determination at this time as there are no allegations or judicially noticeable documents indicating that Petitioner was informed of this decision and when), the Court finds that Petitioner is entitled to equitable relief for failure to timely file and serve the petition; it is apparent from the record before the Court that the delay in filing, issuance and service of summons was at least partially caused by the e-filing requirements and backlog in processing such e-filings. In other words, had the Petitioner been allowed to file the petition at the window on September 23, 2021, the petition would have been filed and summons issued on September 23, 2021 within 90 days of June 29, 2021. Since summons were not issued until October 6, 2021, it was not possible for Petitioner to serve the summons. Prevailing party to submit order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines, Clerk: N. Wong, Reported. (D501)
